message.content:
can you look at this linear ticket:

Context

Related Ticket: STA-1796 

Now that we have customer_health_scores, even this simple initial run of just a single day, in Redshift, we can start building a view that can eventually be used in a CS/CX/Everyone friendly Metabase dashboard

Key Inclusions:

HubSpot CS Lead — key for Daryl, Anna, and Taylor to quickly filter to their customers

Plan Type/Plan Type Group — easy way to filter between self-serve and custom plan deals

Customer Cumulative Metrics — the why behind the predicted NRR

you have access to redshift's public schema.

to start, can you spin up a view that will live in the daily updates folder (not standard or hourly) that starts with customer cumulative metrics and joins in the org name from mysql_organizations, and then also joins in the plan type and plan type category from view_mrr_by_customer_by_day?

content.text: I'll help you create a view that joins customer health scores with organization details and plan information. Let me start by exploring the available schemas and understanding the structure of the tables we need to work with.

-- View: view_customer_health_dashboard_base
-- Description: Base view for customer health dashboard joining cumulative metrics with organization details and plan information

-- Purpose: Foundation for CS/CX/Everyone-friendly Metabase dashboard showing customer health scores and key metrics

CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW public.view_customer_health_dashboard_base AS
SELECT 
    -- Customer and date identifiers
    ccm.customer_id,
    ccm.event_day,
    
    -- Organization details
    org.name AS organization_name,
    
    -- Plan information from MRR view
    mrr.plan_type,

    -- Customer Success Lead from HubSpot deals

    -- Trial and subscription lifecycle
    ccm.is_trial_day,
    ccm.day_number_including_trial_days,
    mrr.cumulative_trial_days,
    
    -- Financial metrics
    ccm.mrr,
    ccm.cumulative_dollars_paid,
    
    -- Usage metrics - Mail
    ccm.cumulative_mail_items_received,
    ccm.cumulative_mail_scans,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_15_days,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_30_days,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_60_days,
    
    -- Usage metrics - Checks
    ccm.cumulative_check_usd,
    ccm.cumulative_check_count,
    ccm.cumulative_check_usd_deposited,
    ccm.cumulative_check_count_deposited,
    ccm.check_count_deposited_past_30_days,
    
    -- Feature adoption metrics
    ccm.cumulative_routing_rules,
    ccm.cumulative_completed_1583_flows,
    ccm.cumulative_reached_1583_stable_signer,
    ccm.cumulative_registered_agent_locations,

    ccm.login_days_past_30_days,
    ccm.login_unique_users_past_15_days,
    ccm.login_unique_users_past_30_days,
    
    -- Team metrics
    ccm.cumulative_users,
    ccm.cumulative_teams,
    
    -- Support metrics
    ccm.cumulative_intercom_conversations,
    ccm.intercom_conversations_past_15_days,

    mrr.churned_mrr,

WHERE 1=1
    -- Filter to only include recent data for performance (can be adjusted based on needs)

;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_customer_health_dashboard_base
PURPOSE: Foundation for CS/CX/Everyone-friendly Metabase dashboard showing customer health scores and key metrics
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a comprehensive view starting with customer health scores and
joining in cumulative metrics, organization details, and plan information. It serves 
as the base for customer health dashboards, enabling CS leads to track their customers' 
health scores and understand the drivers behind predicted NRR.

GRANULARITY:
One row per customer per day. Each customer_id + event_day combination is unique,
based on the customer_health_scores table.

METHODOLOGY:
1. Start with customer_health_scores as the base
2. Join customer cumulative metrics on customer_id and event_day
3. Join MRR information on customer_id and event_day
4. Join organization names from MySQL organizations
5. Include all key metrics needed for customer health analysis

FILTERS:
- Inherits any filters from the base customer_health_scores table
- Additional filtering can be applied at query time

KEY METRICS:
- Health Score: From customer_health_scores (when available)
- Financial: MRR, cumulative dollars paid
- Usage: Mail items, scans, checks
- Engagement: Login days, unique users
- Feature Adoption: Routing rules, 1583 flows, tags
- Support: Intercom conversations

USE CASES:
- Customer health score tracking and analysis
- CS team customer portfolio management  
- Understanding drivers behind predicted NRR
- Churn risk identification
- Feature adoption analysis
- Customer engagement monitoring

DATA QUALITY NOTES:
- Organization names may be NULL if no stripeid match
- Some metrics may be NULL if no matching records in joined tables
- All date/time fields are in UTC unless otherwise specified

COLUMNS:
From customer_health_scores:
- customer_id: Stripe customer ID
- event_day: Date of the metrics snapshot
- (Additional health score columns when implemented)

From mysql_organizations:
- organization_name: Name of the organization

From view_mrr_by_customer_by_day:
- plan_type: Current plan type (e.g., 'grow', 'scale', 'custom')
- plan_type_category: Plan category for easy filtering (self-serve vs custom)
- payment_cadence: Monthly or annual payment frequency
- mrr: Monthly Recurring Revenue on this day
- cumulative_trial_days: Total trial days accumulated
- previous_day_mrr: MRR from previous day
- churned_mrr: MRR lost due to churn
- expansion_mrr: MRR gained from expansion
- new_mrr: New MRR added
- mrr_transferred_in: MRR transferred in from another customer
- mrr_transferred_out: MRR transferred out to another customer

From view_customer_cumulative_metrics:
- is_trial_day: Whether this day is part of trial period
- day_number_including_trial_days: Days since customer start including trial
- cumulative_dollars_paid: Total dollars paid to date
- cumulative_mail_items_received: Total mail items received to date
- cumulative_mail_scans: Total mail scans to date
- mail_scans_past_15_days: Mail scans in past 15 days
- mail_scans_past_30_days: Mail scans in past 30 days
- mail_scans_past_60_days: Mail scans in past 60 days
- cumulative_check_usd: Total check value processed to date
- cumulative_check_count: Total number of checks processed to date
- cumulative_check_usd_deposited: Total check value deposited to date
- cumulative_check_count_deposited: Total number of checks deposited to date
- check_count_deposited_past_30_days: Checks deposited in past 30 days
- cumulative_routing_rules: Total routing rules created to date
- cumulative_completed_1583_flows: Total completed 1583 flows to date
- cumulative_reached_1583_stable_signer: Total reached 1583 stable signer to date
- cumulative_registered_agent_locations: Total registered agent locations to date
- cumulative_mailbox_locations: Total mailbox locations to date
- cumulative_tags: Total tags created to date
- cumulative_rescans: Total rescans requested to date
- rescans_past_30_days: Rescans in past 30 days
- login_days_past_15_days: Days with login activity in past 15 days
- login_days_past_30_days: Days with login activity in past 30 days
- login_unique_users_past_15_days: Unique users who logged in past 15 days
- login_unique_users_past_30_days: Unique users who logged in past 30 days
- cumulative_users: Total users created to date
- cumulative_teams: Total teams created to date
- cumulative_intercom_conversations: Total intercom conversations to date
- intercom_conversations_past_15_days: Intercom conversations in past 15 days
- intercom_conversations_past_30_days: Intercom conversations in past 30 days

FUTURE ENHANCEMENTS:
- Add HubSpot CS Lead when deal data integration is complete
- Include predicted NRR when model outputs are available
- Add additional health score components as they become available

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.customer_health_scores: Base health scores table
- public.view_customer_cumulative_metrics: Cumulative usage and engagement metrics
- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR and plan information
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization names and details

RELATED TICKETS:
- STA-1796: Initial customer health score implementation

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-26
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_customer_health_dashboard_base_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_customer_health_dashboard_base_new
DISTKEY(customer_id)
SORTKEY(event_day, customer_id)
AS
SELECT 
    -- Primary keys from customer_health_scores
    chs.customer_id,
    chs.event_day,
    
    -- Health score and prediction fields
    chs.predicted_nrr,
    chs.model_timestamp,
    chs.model_num_features,
    chs.prediction_horizon_weeks,
    chs.generated_at,
    
    -- Organization details
    org.name AS organization_name,
    
    -- Plan information from MRR view
    mrr.plan_type,
    mrr.plan_type_category,
    mrr.payment_cadence,
    
    -- Customer Success Lead from HubSpot deals
    -- TODO: Add join with HubSpot deals for CS Lead in future iteration
    -- deals.properties_customer_success_lead AS cs_lead,
    
    -- Trial and subscription lifecycle
    ccm.is_trial_day,
    ccm.day_number_including_trial_days,
    mrr.cumulative_trial_days,
    
    -- Financial metrics
    mrr.mrr,
    ccm.cumulative_dollars_paid,
    
    -- Usage metrics - Mail
    ccm.cumulative_mail_items_received,
    ccm.cumulative_mail_scans,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_15_days,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_30_days,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_60_days,
    
    -- Usage metrics - Checks
    ccm.cumulative_check_usd,
    ccm.cumulative_check_count,
    ccm.cumulative_check_usd_deposited,
    ccm.cumulative_check_count_deposited,
    ccm.check_count_deposited_past_30_days,
    
    -- Feature adoption metrics
    ccm.cumulative_routing_rules,
    ccm.cumulative_completed_1583_flows,
    ccm.cumulative_reached_1583_stable_signer,
    ccm.cumulative_registered_agent_locations,
    ccm.cumulative_mailbox_locations,
    ccm.cumulative_tags,
    
    -- Engagement metrics
    ccm.cumulative_rescans,
    ccm.rescans_past_30_days,
    ccm.login_days_past_15_days,
    ccm.login_days_past_30_days,
    ccm.login_unique_users_past_15_days,
    ccm.login_unique_users_past_30_days,
    
    -- Team metrics
    ccm.cumulative_users,
    ccm.cumulative_teams,
    
    -- Support metrics
    ccm.cumulative_intercom_conversations,
    ccm.intercom_conversations_past_15_days,
    ccm.intercom_conversations_past_30_days,
    
    -- MRR movement metrics
    mrr.previous_day_mrr,
    mrr.churned_mrr,
    mrr.expansion_mrr,
    mrr.new_mrr,
    mrr.mrr_transferred_in,
    mrr.mrr_transferred_out
    
FROM public.customer_health_scores chs
LEFT JOIN public.view_customer_cumulative_metrics ccm
    ON chs.customer_id = ccm.customer_id 
    AND chs.event_day = ccm.event_day
LEFT JOIN public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day mrr
    ON chs.customer_id = mrr.customer_id 
    AND chs.event_day = mrr.event_day
LEFT JOIN public.mysql_organizations org 
    ON chs.customer_id = org.stripeid;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_customer_health_dashboard_base;
ALTER TABLE view_customer_health_dashboard_base_new RENAME TO view_customer_health_dashboard_base;

COMMIT;

+- model_timestamp: Timestamp when the model was run
+- model_num_features: Number of features used in the model
+- prediction_horizon_weeks: Number of weeks ahead the prediction is for
+- generated_at: When this prediction was generated

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_customer_health_dashboard_base
PURPOSE: Foundation for CS/CX/Everyone-friendly Metabase dashboard showing customer health scores and key metrics
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides a comprehensive view starting with customer health scores and
joining in cumulative metrics, organization details, and plan information. It serves 
as the base for customer health dashboards, enabling CS leads to track their customers' 
health scores and understand the drivers behind predicted NRR.

GRANULARITY:
One row per customer per day. Each customer_id + event_day combination is unique,
based on the customer_health_scores table.

METHODOLOGY:
1. Start with customer_health_scores as the base
2. Join customer cumulative metrics on customer_id and event_day
3. Join MRR information on customer_id and event_day
4. Join organization names from MySQL organizations
5. Include all key metrics needed for customer health analysis

FILTERS:
- Inherits any filters from the base customer_health_scores table
- Additional filtering can be applied at query time

KEY METRICS:
- Health Score: From customer_health_scores (when available)
- Financial: MRR, cumulative dollars paid
- Usage: Mail items, scans, checks
- Engagement: Login days, unique users
- Feature Adoption: Routing rules, 1583 flows, tags
- Support: Intercom conversations

USE CASES:
- Customer health score tracking and analysis
- CS team customer portfolio management  
- Understanding drivers behind predicted NRR
- Churn risk identification
- Feature adoption analysis
- Customer engagement monitoring

DATA QUALITY NOTES:
- Organization names may be NULL if no stripeid match
- Some metrics may be NULL if no matching records in joined tables
- All date/time fields are in UTC unless otherwise specified

COLUMNS:
From customer_health_scores:
- customer_id: Stripe customer ID
- event_day: Date of the metrics snapshot
- predicted_nrr: Predicted Net Revenue Retention
- model_timestamp: Timestamp when the model was run
- model_num_features: Number of features used in the model
- prediction_horizon_weeks: Number of weeks ahead the prediction is for
- generated_at: When this prediction was generated

From mysql_organizations:
- organization_name: Name of the organization

From view_hubspot_deals_all (via deal_id from MRR view):
- deal_owner_name: Full name of the deal owner from the first closed-won deal
- customer_success_lead_name: Full name of the assigned customer success lead

From view_mrr_by_customer_by_day:
- plan_type: Current plan type (e.g., 'grow', 'scale', 'custom')
- plan_type_category: Plan category for easy filtering (self-serve vs custom)
- payment_cadence: Monthly or annual payment frequency
- mrr: Monthly Recurring Revenue on this day
- cumulative_trial_days: Total trial days accumulated
- previous_day_mrr: MRR from previous day
- churned_mrr: MRR lost due to churn
- expansion_mrr: MRR gained from expansion
- new_mrr: New MRR added
- mrr_transferred_in: MRR transferred in from another customer
- mrr_transferred_out: MRR transferred out to another customer

From view_customer_cumulative_metrics:
- is_trial_day: Whether this day is part of trial period
- day_number_including_trial_days: Days since customer start including trial
- cumulative_dollars_paid: Total dollars paid to date
- cumulative_mail_items_received: Total mail items received to date
- cumulative_mail_scans: Total mail scans to date
- mail_scans_past_15_days: Mail scans in past 15 days
- mail_scans_past_30_days: Mail scans in past 30 days
- mail_scans_past_60_days: Mail scans in past 60 days
- cumulative_check_usd: Total check value processed to date
- cumulative_check_count: Total number of checks processed to date
- cumulative_check_usd_deposited: Total check value deposited to date
- cumulative_check_count_deposited: Total number of checks deposited to date
- check_count_deposited_past_30_days: Checks deposited in past 30 days
- cumulative_routing_rules: Total routing rules created to date
- cumulative_completed_1583_flows: Total completed 1583 flows to date
- cumulative_reached_1583_stable_signer: Total reached 1583 stable signer to date
- cumulative_registered_agent_locations: Total registered agent locations to date
- cumulative_mailbox_locations: Total mailbox locations to date
- cumulative_tags: Total tags created to date
- cumulative_rescans: Total rescans requested to date
- rescans_past_30_days: Rescans in past 30 days
- login_days_past_15_days: Days with login activity in past 15 days
- login_days_past_30_days: Days with login activity in past 30 days
- login_unique_users_past_15_days: Unique users who logged in past 15 days
- login_unique_users_past_30_days: Unique users who logged in past 30 days
- cumulative_users: Total users created to date
- cumulative_teams: Total teams created to date
- cumulative_intercom_conversations: Total intercom conversations to date
- intercom_conversations_past_15_days: Intercom conversations in past 15 days
- intercom_conversations_past_30_days: Intercom conversations in past 30 days

FUTURE ENHANCEMENTS:
- Add HubSpot CS Lead when deal data integration is complete
- Include predicted NRR when model outputs are available
- Add additional health score components as they become available

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.customer_health_scores: Base health scores table
- public.view_customer_cumulative_metrics: Cumulative usage and engagement metrics
- public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day: MRR and plan information
- public.mysql_organizations: Organization names and details

RELATED TICKETS:
- STA-1796: Initial customer health score implementation

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-26
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_customer_health_dashboard_base_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_customer_health_dashboard_base_new
DISTKEY(customer_id)
SORTKEY(event_day, customer_id)
AS
SELECT 
    -- Primary keys from customer_health_scores
    chs.customer_id,
    chs.event_day,
    
    -- Health score and prediction fields
    chs.predicted_nrr,
    chs.model_timestamp,
    chs.model_num_features,
    chs.prediction_horizon_weeks,
    chs.generated_at,
    
    -- Organization details
    org.name AS organization_name,
    
    -- Plan information from MRR view
    mrr.plan_type,
    mrr.plan_type_category,
    mrr.payment_cadence,
    
    -- Deal owner and CS Lead from HubSpot deals (using deal_id from MRR view)
    deals.deal_owner_name,
    deals.customer_success_lead_name,
    
    -- Trial and subscription lifecycle
    ccm.is_trial_day,
    ccm.day_number_including_trial_days,
    mrr.cumulative_trial_days,
    
    -- Financial metrics
    mrr.mrr,
    ccm.cumulative_dollars_paid,
    
    -- Usage metrics - Mail
    ccm.cumulative_mail_items_received,
    ccm.cumulative_mail_scans,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_15_days,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_30_days,
    ccm.mail_scans_past_60_days,
    
    -- Usage metrics - Checks
    ccm.cumulative_check_usd,
    ccm.cumulative_check_count,
    ccm.cumulative_check_usd_deposited,
    ccm.cumulative_check_count_deposited,
    ccm.check_count_deposited_past_30_days,
    
    -- Feature adoption metrics
    ccm.cumulative_routing_rules,
    ccm.cumulative_completed_1583_flows,
    ccm.cumulative_reached_1583_stable_signer,
    ccm.cumulative_registered_agent_locations,
    ccm.cumulative_mailbox_locations,
    ccm.cumulative_tags,
    
    -- Engagement metrics
    ccm.cumulative_rescans,
    ccm.rescans_past_30_days,
    ccm.login_days_past_15_days,
    ccm.login_days_past_30_days,
    ccm.login_unique_users_past_15_days,
    ccm.login_unique_users_past_30_days,
    
    -- Team metrics
    ccm.cumulative_users,
    ccm.cumulative_teams,
    
    -- Support metrics
    ccm.cumulative_intercom_conversations,
    ccm.intercom_conversations_past_15_days,
    ccm.intercom_conversations_past_30_days,
    
    -- MRR movement metrics
    mrr.previous_day_mrr,
    mrr.churned_mrr,
    mrr.expansion_mrr,
    mrr.new_mrr,
    mrr.mrr_transferred_in,
    mrr.mrr_transferred_out
    
FROM public.customer_health_scores chs
LEFT JOIN public.view_customer_cumulative_metrics ccm
    ON chs.customer_id = ccm.customer_id 
    AND chs.event_day = ccm.event_day
LEFT JOIN public.view_mrr_by_customer_by_day mrr
    ON chs.customer_id = mrr.customer_id 
    AND chs.event_day = mrr.event_day
LEFT JOIN public.mysql_organizations org 
    ON chs.customer_id = org.stripeid
LEFT JOIN public.view_hubspot_deals_all deals
    ON mrr.deal_id = deals.deal_id;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_customer_health_dashboard_base;
ALTER TABLE view_customer_health_dashboard_base_new RENAME TO view_customer_health_dashboard_base;

COMMIT;

/*
================================================================================
TABLE: view_customer_health_base_latest
PURPOSE: Latest snapshot of customer health metrics showing only the most recent event_day per customer
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides the most recent health score and metrics for each customer,
selecting only the latest event_day from the base customer health dashboard.
This is optimized for current-state dashboards where historical trends are not needed.

GRANULARITY:
One row per customer (customer_id is unique). Each customer appears exactly once
with their most recent metrics snapshot.

METHODOLOGY:
1. Window function to identify the max event_day for each customer
2. Filter to only include rows where event_day = max_event_day
3. All metrics represent the customer's current state

FILTERS:
- Only includes the most recent event_day for each customer
- Inherits all joins and logic from view_customer_health_dashboard_base

KEY METRICS:
Same as view_customer_health_dashboard_base:
- Predicted NRR and health score metrics
- Current MRR and plan information
- Latest cumulative usage metrics
- Most recent engagement metrics
- Current CS lead and deal owner

USE CASES:
- Current customer portfolio dashboards
- CS team daily/weekly reviews
- Executive dashboards showing current state
- Alerting on current health scores
- Quick lookups of customer current status

DATA QUALITY NOTES:
- Assumes customer_health_scores will be updated regularly with new event_days
- Latest data depends on the refresh schedule of underlying tables
- NULL values possible for customers without matching records in joined tables

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.view_customer_health_dashboard_base: Base table with all historical data

RELATED TICKETS:
- STA-1796: Initial customer health score implementation

/*

TABLE: view_customer_health_base_latest
PURPOSE: Latest snapshot of customer health metrics showing only the most recent event_day per customer
================================================================================

DESCRIPTION:
This table provides the most recent health score and metrics for each customer,
selecting only the latest event_day from the base customer health dashboard.
This is optimized for current-state dashboards where historical trends are not needed.

GRANULARITY:
One row per customer (customer_id is unique). Each customer appears exactly once
with their most recent metrics snapshot.

METHODOLOGY:
1. Window function to identify the max event_day for each customer
2. Filter to only include rows where event_day = max_event_day
3. All metrics represent the customer's current state

FILTERS:
- Only includes the most recent event_day for each customer
- Inherits all joins and logic from view_customer_health_dashboard_base

KEY METRICS:
Same as view_customer_health_dashboard_base:
- Predicted NRR and health score metrics
- Current MRR and plan information
- Latest cumulative usage metrics
- Most recent engagement metrics
- Current CS lead and deal owner

USE CASES:
- Current customer portfolio dashboards
- CS team daily/weekly reviews
- Executive dashboards showing current state
- Alerting on current health scores
- Quick lookups of customer current status

DATA QUALITY NOTES:
- Assumes customer_health_scores will be updated regularly with new event_days
- Latest data depends on the refresh schedule of underlying tables
- NULL values possible for customers without matching records in joined tables

DEPENDENCIES:
- public.view_customer_health_dashboard_base: Base table with all historical data

RELATED TICKETS:
- STA-1796: Initial customer health score implementation

LAST MODIFIED: 2026-02-26
OWNER: Data Team
================================================================================
*/

-- Step 1: Drop the _new table if it exists (cleanup from any previous failed runs)
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_customer_health_base_latest_new;

-- Step 2: Create the new table with fresh data
CREATE TABLE view_customer_health_base_latest_new
DISTKEY(customer_id)
SORTKEY(customer_id)
AS
WITH latest_dates AS (
    -- Find the most recent event_day for each customer
    SELECT 
        customer_id,
        MAX(event_day) AS max_event_day
    FROM public.view_customer_health_dashboard_base
    GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT 
    base.*
FROM public.view_customer_health_dashboard_base base
INNER JOIN latest_dates ld
    ON base.customer_id = ld.customer_id 
    AND base.event_day = ld.max_event_day;

-- Step 3: Atomic swap - drop old and rename new
BEGIN;

DROP TABLE IF EXISTS view_customer_health_base_latest;
ALTER TABLE view_customer_health_base_latest_new RENAME TO view_customer_health_base_latest;

COMMIT;
